title: Closure created inside named-let loop that set!s a GLOBAL loses the mutation
goal: Found by the P4 stress harness (2026-07-02, master 2692ca63). A lambda created in a named-let loop body that set!s a global variable silently drops every write: (define c 0) + loop body (call-it (lambda () (set! c (+ c 1)))) leaves c=0 after 3 iterations, JIT and AOT. The identical program without the loop prints 3. Bug-RR / PR#50 family: #50 fixed set!-mutated OUTER-LOCAL captures in named-let; globals referenced from a loop-body lambda still go through a stale copy. Consequence: any mutex-serialized shared counter incremented from a worker loop stays 0 (tests/stress/found/serialized_counter_10k.esk).
acceptance: budgets.tsv rows promoted from XKNOWN to normal (harness XPASS turns green)
campaign_payoff: Silent wrong-value on the shared-mutable-state + loop composition; blocks the v1.3 serialized-counter parallel scenario.
